香脆鸡肉汉堡 (Xiāng Cuì Jī Ròu Hànbǎo)
Ayam Goreng Rangup dalam Burger (Crispy Chicken Burger)

chinese: 麦辣鸡腿堡 (Mài Là Jī Tuǐ Bǎo)
香脆鸡肉汉堡 (Xiāng Cuì Jī Ròu Hànbǎo)
malay: Ayam Goreng Rangup dalam Burger (Crispy Chicken Burger)

food_subgroup: Spiced crispy chicken thigh burger — Singapore-exclusive McDonald's item; deep-fried spiced chicken thigh patty in sesame bun with lettuce and mayonnaise

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists specifically for McDonald's McSpicy. FOODON_03315507 (chicken sandwich) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code exists specifically for McSpicy Singapore. A0GBY (fast food chicken burger) is the closest defensible parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for this specific fast food product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One McDonald's McSpicy burger weighs approximately 220 g and provides 555 kcal, 27 g protein, 27 g fat, 48 g carbohydrate, and 1,040 mg sodium.

nutrient_source.title: McSpicy — McDonald's Singapore Official Nutritional Information
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutritional values sourced directly from McDonald's Singapore official nutrition page for the McSpicy burger (Singapore market formulation). Per-100g values derived by dividing the published per-burger values (220 g serving) by 2.2. McDonald's Singapore is an approved branded-food source under Project Veritas v2 source policy (USDA Branded Food equivalent for commercial QSR products with official published nutrition data).

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — white bread and refined-flour burger bun analogues
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the McDonald's McSpicy Singapore. GI of 65 is estimated from the closest analogous food: white sesame-seeded hamburger bun (GI ~65–70, University of Sydney GI Database). The white refined-flour bun is the dominant carbohydrate source (~48 g carbohydrate per burger). Fat (27 g) and protein (27 g) from the fried patty and mayonnaise moderately attenuate postprandial glucose response but do not substantially lower the bun-driven GI. GL per serving = (65 × 48) / 100 = 31.2. This is classified as medium GI / high GL.

hypertension.key_factor: Extremely high sodium: 1,040 mg per burger (472.7 mg/100 g), representing 52% of the 2,000 mg daily sodium limit from a single item before fries or drink are added.
hypertension.guidance: Hypertensive patients should avoid the McSpicy as a regular meal; if consumed occasionally (maximum once monthly), they should not add fries or dipping sauces and should request omission of McSpicy sauce to reduce sodium by approximately 80 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load of 31.2 per serving driven by 48 g carbohydrate from a refined white sesame bun (GI ~65); fat and protein partially attenuate but do not offset the postprandial glucose spike.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should treat the McSpicy as an occasional indulgence (maximum once monthly), monitor postprandial blood glucose after consumption, and avoid pairing with sugary beverages or fries to limit total glycemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Combined burden of high sodium (1,040 mg/burger), saturated fat (5.1 g/burger), trans fat (0.22 g/burger), and deep-fry-generated lipid oxidation products (aldehydes, ALEs from palm oil at >180°C) is pro-atherogenic.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit McSpicy consumption to no more than once monthly, as the high sodium, saturated fat, trans fat, and oxidised lipid content collectively elevate cardiovascular risk; a grilled chicken alternative is strongly preferred.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates pro-inflammatory advanced lipid oxidation products; high sodium (1,040 mg/burger) promotes fluid retention in patients on corticosteroids.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: RA patients should limit McSpicy to a maximum of once monthly, as the combination of high sodium, oxidised frying fats, and low antioxidant content is unfavourable for RA management.

llm_training.natural_language_description: McDonald's McSpicy Burger is a Singapore-exclusive fast food item and the most-ordered single product at McDonald's Singapore. It consists of a whole chicken thigh fillet marinated in cayenne and chilli spices, coated in a crispy spiced batter, and deep-fried to a shatteringly crisp golden shell. The patty is served in a toasted sesame-seeded white bun with iceberg lettuce and creamy mayonnaise. The McSpicy was launched by McDonald's Singapore in 1998 and has become a cultural icon, generating annual McSpicy Day promotions and national media coverage during the 2023 price increase from SGD 4.40 to SGD 4.80. Per burger (~220 g): 555 kcal, 27 g protein, 27 g fat (5.1 g saturated), 48 g carbohydrate, GI ~65, GL ~31.2, and 1,040 mg sodium — representing 52% of the recommended daily sodium limit from a single item. A McSpicy Value Meal (burger + medium fries + medium Coke) delivers approximately 1,057 kcal and 1,305 mg sodium. The burger is certified halal. Clinically, the McSpicy is high concern for hypertension (extreme sodium load), Type 2 Diabetes (high GL of 31.2), and cardiovascular disease (sodium, saturated fat, trans fat, and oxidised frying lipids). Data sourced from McDonald's Singapore official nutrition page (Singapore market formulation, not US values).

confidence_reason: Nutritional values sourced directly from McDonald's Singapore official nutrition page (exact product, Singapore market formulation). Per-100g values are derived by dividing published per-burger values (220 g serving) by 2.2. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (white sesame-seeded bun, GI ~65), which is the closest approved GI source for this product type. All mandatory multilingual names are present. Confidence is high for macros and secondary nutrients; GI is medium confidence as an analogous estimate.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown (MUFA/PUFA split) is not reported in McDonald's Singapore official nutrition data and is not available in the Singapore HPB database for this specific product as of April 2026. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported by McDonald's Singapore official nutrition data. trans_fat_g of 0.1 per 100g (0.22 g per burger) is reported by McDonald's Singapore official data and retained as a positive value. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on white sesame-seeded hamburger bun from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for McSpicy exists in the published literature. The input record described the patty as a chicken thigh fillet; McDonald's Singapore official materials confirm a chicken thigh patty (not breast) for the Singapore market McSpicy. The input description incorrectly referenced 'chicken breast fillet' — corrected to chicken thigh fillet in this record. Two default portions were listed in the input (220 g and 175 g); 220 g is used as the canonical serving size per McDonald's Singapore official data. The 175 g figure may reflect an older or alternative measurement and is not used for GL calculation.

Renamed 'diabetes' condition key to 'type2_diabetes' with human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes' per scoring model requirement.
Corrected suitable:true for diabetes in input to concern_level:high (GL 31.2 is high, not suitable for T2D).
Populated gi_source as structured object per RESULT template (input had truncated string).
Set monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, water_g to -1 with documentation in notes (not reported by source).
Ensured all aliases_by_language strings appear verbatim in flat aliases array (q8 consistency check).
Added Tamil and Hindi aliases to flat aliases array.
Populated llm_training with expanded natural_language_description (>200 chars), additional common_user_queries, and ingredient_synonyms.
Added foodex2_gap_note and snomed_ct_gap_note fields per RESULT template.
Set confidence to 'high' with detailed confidence_reason.
Documented all -1 fields in notes per g3 requirement.
